title: benthamidia florida f. pluribracteata
intro: Benthamidia florida f. pluribracteata, also known as the Philippine mahagon or Philippine rosewood, is a plant species belonging to the family Magnoliaceae. Traditionally, it is used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Benthamidia florida f. pluribracteata can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, although more research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy as a medicinal remedy.
